In original mode the HQ pipeline merges the stage-1 distilled LoRA into the DiT per request and unmerges it to switch to

sglang.title: Fix IndexCache PP topk handoff
sglang.author: mmangkad
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fixes #28525.
This fixes a PP case in IndexCache where GLM-5.2 can start a PP stage on a layer that reuses top-k from the previous layer.
With pp-size=2, rank 1 starts at layer 39. For GLM-5.2, layer 38 computes top-k and layer 39 reuses it. Since the current PP proxy only passed hidden_stat

SGLang engine records sglang:time_to_first_token_seconds on a different
grid (0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6,

The current parameter injection logic injects rope_theta to rope_scaling dict for all configurations that rope_theta is not in

Enable HiSparse for DSA models on ROCm
Summary
This change enables HiSparse for DeepSeek-style DSA models on ROCm, validated on MI355X, while keeping CUDA behavior unchanged. ROCm uses TileLang as the default HiSparse DSA backend, supports FP8 and BF16 KV cache, and can also run with AITER when the

sglang.title: [AMD] fix(jit): port kv_canary write/verify/plan kernels to ROCm

Make the kv_canary JIT kernels (write, verify, plan_entries) compile and run correctly under hipcc/ROCm, and onboard their JIT unit tests to the jit-kernel-unit-test-amd suite. All ROCm-specific behavior is gated behind USE_ROCM so the CUDA/NV path is unchanged. This unblocks the kv_canary e

The 8 GB300 tests under test/registered/gb300/ and the nightly-4-gpu-gb300 suite were added in #21487, but no workflow job was ever added to dispatch them — so they only ran via a separ

Summary
Unify weight-update runner fanout via a per-worker abstraction; extend begin/end to the draft
Motivation
The scheduler reached weight-update runners two asymmetric ways — tp_worker.model_runner for the target, draft_worker.iter_draft_runners() for the draft — and begin_weight_update / end_we

sglang.title: refactor(runner): add EagerRunner, own the eager path, polymorphic dispatch
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The eager forward path lived inline in ModelRunner. Extract it into a dedicated runner so the cuda-graph runners stay purely cuda-graph, and back it with one fixed-size static input buffer (allocated once at init) instead of grow-on-demand registries.
Modifications

New runner/eager_runne

sglang.title: refactor(runner): move kernel warmup into the shared runner lifecycle (warmup())
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Kernel warmup / flashinfer autotune belongs to the runner lifecycle and should run at init, not as a standalone ModelRunner step or in the forward path.
Modifications

BaseRunner.warmup() holds the orchestration (run-once guard, device guard, flashinfer allreduce pre-init, flashinfer auto

sglang.title: refactor(runner): reuse a prepared static buffer for every dummy run
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The flashinfer-autotune dummy forward allocated a throwaway DecodeInputBuffers set sized to req_to_token_pool.size. Reuse the runner's already-allocated static buffers instead.
Modifications

_dummy_run always runs over a caller-prepared static buffer — it asserts one is given, never allo

sglang.title: fix(runner): size eager static buffers for prefill budget and MLP-sync autotune
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
The eager runner's static buffers were under-sized on two independent axes.
1. Token axis — the profiler hang
The prefill buffer was sized to chunked_prefill_size. That is the correct cap for normal chunked prefill (PrefillAdder admits against the remaining chunk budget), but under PP --enab

Bumps all actions/github-script@v6/v7 usages to @v8 to silence the Node 20 deprecation warning (v8 runs on Node 24). Pure version bump; the scripts only use stable github.rest.* / github.paginate / core.* / context.* APIs.

Motivation
On the AMD CI runner-fleet report, linux-mi35x-gpu-8 is badly saturated — it is the only "big" mi35x pool, so almost every mi35x job lands on it (peak/queue dominated by it, P99 queue measured around ~20h), while linux-mi35x-gpu-1 / linux-mi35x-gpu-2 sit nearly idle and linux-mi35x-gpu-4

sglang.title: [AMD] register 3 tests to stage-b-test-1-gpu-large-amd (batch-6)
sglang.author: michaelzhang-ai
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Batch-6 of the NV→AMD CI coverage audit. Registers 3 backend-agnostic / triton-path base-b-test-1-gpu-large CUDA tests to the AMD lane (stage-b-test-1-gpu-large-amd), AMD est ≈ 1.5× CUDA. All 3 verified green on both AMD lanes (mi325 + rocm720) via continue_on_error dispatch on the PR head.

sglang.title: [router] Raise chat body cap to 5 MiB for long contexts
sglang.author: Kangyan-Zhou
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The experimental router caps the /v1/chat/completions request body at 1 MiB (MAX_CHAT_BODY_BYTES). A long context — up to ~1M tokens — tokenized as JSON exceeds 1 MiB, so legitimate long-context requests were rejected with 413 PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE before reaching a worker.
Changes

Raise MAX

sglang serve --model-path inclusionAI/Ring-2.5-1T --trust-remote-code --load-format dummy --tp 8

The error message:
File "/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/models

sglang.title: [router] Tokenize prompt once at ingress; forward input_ids to the engine (all policies)
sglang.author: Kangyan-Zhou
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
What
sgl-router with --policy cache_aware_zmq tokenizes the prompt to compute its routing hashes, while the engine separately tokenizes the same prompt again for inference. For long-context requests (55k–125k+ tokens) that double tokenization is a large, redundant cost, and the router also re-parsed

flashinfer 0.6.12 incompatibility. The vendored TRT-LLM fused-MoE JIT kernels were forked from flashinfer 0

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fall back to Pydantic loc for param in validation errors
vllm.author: muhammadfawaz1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, param was only populated for errors carrying a custom VLLMValidationError in their Pydantic ctx, leaving it None for ordinary missing-field/wrong-type validation errors even though the field name was available from error['loc'].
Purpose
Fixes the param field in validation error responses

vllm.title: [12/n]  final _C library kernel migration
vllm.author: cleonard530
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR continues the libtorch stable ABI migration (see #26946) for vLLM and is the final _C library kernels to move to the _C_stable_libtorch library. The PR moves csrc/quantization/activation_kernels.cu to csrc/libtorch_stable/quantization/activation_kernels.cu, along with the weak_ref_te

vllm.title: [DSv4] Pack KV caches into contiguous per-block allocations for DeepSeek V4
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
For DeepSeek V4, pack all layer data contiguously per block so that KV connectors can send/receive one region per block.
Full-attention MLA + SWA/compressor caches share one contiguous allocation per block. Each layer gets an as_strided view with storage_offset into the packed backing tensor.
The pa

vllm.title: [Test] Migrate test_openai_schema.py to schemathesis 4.x
vllm.author: bbrowning
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Follow-up to #45675 which upgraded schemathesis to 4.x for a security fix. That PR updated the pinned versions but not the test code, which uses APIs that changed across the major version boundary.
In addition to migrating to the new APIs, this also had to bump the schemathesis lower bound t

vllm.title: [ROCm] Use vLLM's fp8 quant max in AITER hipBLASLt accuracy test
vllm.author: djramic
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes test_hipb_mm_kernel_forward_accuracy on MI300. The test quantized its fp8 reference with torch.finfo(e4m3fnuz).max (240.0), while the kernel uses vLLM's get_fp8_min_max(), which on fnuz intentionally
returns 224.0. That ~7% scale mismatch gave max_diff ≈ 10 and tripped the strict asser

Add Buildkite CI-failure diagnosis skill

Moves the Buildkite CI-failure diagnosis instructions out of AGENTS.md into a dedicated .claude/skills/ci-fails-buildkite skill, and updates supporting config so the skill is tracked.
Also refreshes CODEOWNERS for the GDN linear-attention path. Was r

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Pin `test_rocm_compressed_tensors_w8a8` to TRITON_ATTN
vllm.author: micah-wil
vllm.bodyExcerpt: AMD: Kernels Quantization Test 2 (mi325_1) is failing on main ever since #44446 was merged. There is a seg fault in the ROCM_ATTN backend, so we're pinning to TRITON_ATTN to unblock CI while I investigate the fix (see #46179).

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Parser] Fix U+FFFD leak at reasoning-to-content transition in engine parsers
vllm.author: bbrowning
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Flush the reasoning parser's engine lexer at the reasoning→content transition instead of decoding isolated token IDs, which produces U+FFFD when byte-fallback tokens span delta boundaries.
This fixes a source of unicode replacement characters getting streamed back to clients first identified

vllm.title: [ROCm] Fix VRAM not freed in test_phi3v
vllm.author: djramic
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
test_phi3v.py::test_models_image fails on ROCm CI (MI300) with:
ValueError: Memory of devices devices=[0] not free after dur_s=240.04 (threshold='0.100')
The HF reference forward in _run_test runs without disabling autograd. This keeps activations alive, so HfRunner.__exit__ can't free the V

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix gridDim.y overflow for large row counts
vllm.author: JasonLi314
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #45099. Prior code mixed up cuda gridDim.x and gridDim.y, mapping the row dimension (mn) to gridDim.y, which CUDA caps at 65535. This fix:

Assigns row dimension mn to gridDim.x instead of gridDim.y, as grid x limit is capped at 2^31 - 1.
Fixed the launch guard, which previously checke
